name: Satellite & Mobile-Data Poverty Mapping
description: This domain covers computational methods for estimating poverty and wealth from non-traditional data sources, particularly satellite imagery and mobile phone records, as seen in readings like "Combining satellite imagery and machine learning to predict poverty" and "Predicting Poverty and Wealth from Mobile Phone Data." It addresses technical approaches such as transfer learning, object detection, and interpretable mapping, alongside applied cases like targeting villages for rural development and cash transfers in Togo. It also engages critical concerns including fairness, urban-rural disparities, and how often prediction models must be updated to remain accurate.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Blumenstock CDR poverty work and the Togo phone-sharing study treat mobile-operator call/SMS logs and tower locations as the raw data source without explaining what fields a CDR contains or how location is inferred from cell towers.

suggested_resources.title: Mobile Phone Network Data for Development: How Analysis of Call Detail Records (CDRs) Provide Valuable Information (ALNAP)
Call detail record (Wikipedia overview)

background_concepts.concept: Transfer learning with CNNs / feature extraction
background_concepts.why_needed: Xie and Jean build poverty predictors by taking ImageNet-pretrained convolutional networks and repurposing them for satellite imagery, assuming the reader understands pretraining, fine-tuning, and using a CNN as a fixed feature extractor.

suggested_resources.title: CS231n — Transfer Learning (Stanford lecture notes)
A Gentle Introduction to Transfer Learning for Deep Learning

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights as an economic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Jean's method uses satellite nighttime luminosity as a training signal for daytime imagery, presuming familiarity with how brightness pixels correlate with economic activity and their known saturation/rural limitations.

suggested_resources.title: Illuminating Economic Growth Using Satellite Images (IMF Finance & Development)

background_concepts.why_needed: The fairness-in-satellite-poverty-maps paper and the interpretable-maps work assume knowledge of fairness harms, group disparities, and how bias enters an ML pipeline (e.g., urban-rural error imbalance).

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to Algorithmic Fairness (lecture notes)

name: Mobile Money & Digital Financial Inclusion
description: This domain examines the adoption, impact, and limitations of mobile money and digital financial services in low- and middle-income countries, drawing on randomized control trials in Afghanistan and systematic reviews of financial services in poorer economies. Readings explore risk sharing after natural disasters, state-led inclusion models, and how underserved microentrepreneurs domesticate mobile payments. It also considers the broader roles of mobile money agents as general ICT intermediaries beyond finance, as in the Tanzania study.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Afghanistan mobile-money study and the systematic reviews of mobile financial services report treatment/control effects and average treatment effects without explaining randomized-evaluation design, which underpins their causal claims.

suggested_resources.title: A Beginner's Guide to Randomized Evaluations in Development Economics
An Interactive Introduction to Randomized Control Trials (SERC/Carleton)

background_concepts.concept: Risk sharing / informal insurance after shocks
background_concepts.why_needed: The risk-sharing-and-mobile-phones paper hinges on the economics concept that households smooth consumption via transfer networks after disasters, a mechanism the paper uses but does not teach.

suggested_resources.title: Illuminating Economic Growth Using Satellite Images (IMF F&D) — for context on shock measurement

name: Microfinance & Alternative Credit Provision
description: This domain traces microfinance from its origins, including Muhammad Yunus's founding vision, through contemporary practice as documented in the Bharat Microfinance Report 2016 and studies of borrower creditworthiness in India's urban slums. It examines lending mechanisms, credit scoring alternatives, and the influence of politics on credit access through works like "Do Lenders Favor Politically Connected Firms?" It also probes marketing and consumer credit dynamics via field experiments on advertising content.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Bharat Microfinance Report, the creditworthiness case study, and the Grameen video assume the reader knows how group/solidarity lending and joint liability substitute for collateral to enable lending to the poor.

suggested_resources.title: Solidarity lending (overview of joint-liability microfinance)

background_concepts.why_needed: The scoping review of alternative credit scoring assumes familiarity with using non-traditional data and ML classifiers to assess creditworthiness and the fairness concerns this raises.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to Algorithmic Fairness
Algorithms for Fair Machine Learning: An Introduction

name: Mobile Phones for Livelihoods & Market Information
description: This domain investigates how mobile phones affect economic activity and market access, anchored by the influential study of India's fishing industry and its critical rebuttal emphasizing complex social context. It explores how diverse users—women livestock keepers in Zimbabwe, Nigerian micro business owners, and emergent smartphone users in India—actually adopt and use mobile technology for their livelihoods. Attention is given to gender gaps and the practical limitations users face despite the sophistication of available tools.

background_concepts.concept: Market information and the law of one price
background_concepts.why_needed: The Kerala fishing study and its critique argue about how phone access reduces price dispersion across markets, assuming the reader grasps arbitrage and price-convergence logic that the papers debate rather than define.

suggested_resources.title: Mobile phones and economic development context — Illuminating Economic Growth (IMF F&D)

name: Digital Tools for Women's Economic Empowerment
description: This domain focuses on designing and deploying digital and appropriate technologies to economically empower women, especially in rural India, through case studies of Indian women micro-entrepreneurs and smartphone use for rural women. Readings cover user-centered design of tools like a seasonal calendar app and skills-based interventions such as coastal seaweed farming training in Tamil Nadu. It also addresses measuring digital literacy in low-experience populations and appropriate innovations like the pedal-powered washing machine.

background_concepts.why_needed: The seasonal-calendar-app and empowerment studies rely on user-centered design cycles (needs assessment, prototyping, iteration) as their methodological backbone without introducing the practice to non-HCI readers.

suggested_resources.title: Fairness & Algorithmic Decision Making — Introduction (for the analysis-of-pipeline framing)

name: Climate Resilience & Community Natural Resource Management
description: This domain addresses community-based approaches to climate adaptation and natural resource management, featuring participatory tools like the "CoDriVE-Programme Designer" handbook and the Commons Connect application for vulnerability evaluation. It incorporates landscape ecology concepts, retrospective resilience assessments of semi-arid Indian farming systems, and community consultation methods. The domain also connects poverty and inequality to local natural resource dynamics, including the local natural resource curse.

background_concepts.concept: Climate vulnerability = exposure + sensitivity + adaptive capacity
background_concepts.why_needed: The CoDriVE vulnerability tool and the semi-arid resilience assessment build indices on the IPCC exposure/sensitivity/adaptive-capacity framework, referencing these components as established terminology.

suggested_resources.title: Vulnerability Assessment (USDA Climate Hubs)
Climate Vulnerability and Risk Assessment: Framework, Methods and Guidelines (IISc/IHCAP)

background_concepts.why_needed: The landscape-ecological-concepts planning paper applies patch/matrix/connectivity metrics and fragmentation indices as if the reader already knows this quantitative landscape-ecology vocabulary.

suggested_resources.title: Applying landscape ecological concepts — Exposure/Sensitivity framing via Climate Change Academy methods guide

name: Smallholder Agriculture & Rural Innovation Livelihoods
description: This domain explores grassroots innovation, entrepreneurship, and livelihood enhancement among smallholder farmers and rural communities, drawing on stories of India's hidden inventors, barefoot movements, and rescuing dancing bears. It examines farm size and productivity debates, effectuation theory in rural entrepreneurship, and integrated agriculture-clean energy solutions in villages like Sadivayal. It also considers how small farmers organize through social movement strategies to build collective agency.

background_concepts.why_needed: The rural-innovation-and-entrepreneurship paper analyzes founders through the lens of effectuation (means-driven, affordable-loss decision logic) without introducing this named theory.

suggested_resources.title: Fairness & Algorithmic Decision Making — Introduction (general reasoning-framework primer)

background_concepts.why_needed: Chand's paper is organized around the long-standing 'inverse relationship' between farm size and per-hectare productivity, a debated agricultural-economics regularity it references as background.

suggested_resources.title: A Beginner's Guide to Randomized Evaluations in Development Economics (for the impact-measurement grounding)

name: Data & Analytics for Aid and SDG Tracking
description: This domain covers the use of open data, analytics, and classification methods for monitoring international aid, social protection, and progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals. Readings include tools for tracking aid contributions to SDGs, norm-based approaches tackling poverty through anti-discrimination, and analyses of COVID-19's impact on vulnerable groups in India. It also features frameworks for mapping vulnerability among disadvantaged populations and reasoning over longitudinal data to estimate livelihoods in lagging rural regions.

background_concepts.why_needed: The aid-tracking and norm-optimization papers classify flows and progress against Sustainable Development Goal targets and indicators, assuming knowledge of the 17-goal, ~230-indicator UN framework.

suggested_resources.title: SDG Indicators (UN Statistics Division)
E-Handbook on Sustainable Development Goals Indicators (UNESCAP)

name: Satellite Imagery and Remote Sensing for Poverty and Socioeconomic Measurement
description: This domain applies remote sensing and satellite imagery to measure socioeconomic conditions and economic activity, including temporal prediction of socio-economic indicators and testing whether medium-resolution Landsat imagery can capture activity at small geographies in Vietnam. It draws on near real-time global land use and land cover mapping tools like Dynamic World. It also extends to related forecasting applications such as ultra-short-term photovoltaic power prediction using multi-layer cloud coverage data.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Dynamic World paper produces near-real-time 10 m land-cover class maps, and Goldblatt uses medium-resolution imagery to detect built-up area, both assuming familiarity with per-pixel land-cover classification and spatial resolution.

suggested_resources.title: ML | Transfer Learning with Convolutional Neural Networks (image classification foundations)

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights / medium-resolution imagery as economic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Goldblatt asks whether medium-resolution imagery can measure economic activity at small geographies, building on the established use of remotely-sensed signals (including night lights) as GDP proxies.

suggested_resources.title: Illuminating Economic Growth Using Satellite Images (IMF F&D)
Measuring quarterly economic growth from outer space (World Bank Blogs)
